1x FLA at the Marne

1x FLA at South Dock

0820 
Class Formation (Uniform: RUCK, PACKING LIST, HELMET, ALL PAPERS FILLED OUT)

1. All meals are coordinated by the Detail S4 and/or rep or OIC for MDS (Air Assault, AMT, SLE, SGR, CIVMX).  Meals are locked in two weeks out which gives the CM enough time to ensure they can adequately support every requirement. Standardized times will be adhered to whenever possible IOT limit confusion and disruptions to the wait staff's 'battle rhythm'

2. Changes to the approved schedule will be coordinated through the S3s/OICs with DMI4 during log synch meetings. Any change requested during the week of execution (7 days out) must be justified by the the Detail S3/4 and approved by Dep Comm or higher.  Any change requested between 7 and 14 days must be approved by DMI6.  Any change 15 - 21 days requires RTO approval.

3. Guests are unauthorized during CST unless approved by the USCC Chief of Staff. Current exceptions include TACs/Detail Staff and TF Medics assigned to each company. Detail S4s are responsible for providing a guest roster NLT Day 4 of LTP/TTA. All guests are responsible for paying for their own meals.

4. Football and basketball teams are authorized team tables during CST to facilitate heavy portions. The Cadet S4 staff will work these tables into the seating charts. The Cadet Mess will ensure that any meals consumed outside of the Mess Hall include additional portions for these Cadets. It is a responsibility of the Cadet leadership to ensure that the proper Cadets receive the additional food and supplements

5. Due to the fact that headcounts at SGR change nearly every meal, the SGR Cadet S4 will provide head count information directly to Ms. Voltaire. The Detail S4s (CLDT, CFT, CBT, SLE) will populate the Mess Matrix for their details respecfully.
6. Ice- Cadets are authorized access ice machines at the Cadet Mess and Okinawa Hall daily to refill ice coolers used for ice sheeting only. Access is 24/7
7. Exceptions to established standards will be included as notes for the week affected
8. MERMITES FOR CLDT: MERMITES will be palletized and marked by company and delivered to Okinawa Mess Hall dock at 0630 and 1800.  Mermites can not sit for more than 3 hours.
8. Supplements for MRE include 1 Gatorade and 1 piece of fruit per person.  Supplements will be delivered on specified days to Okinawa Hall and stored in the refridgerators in OH.
9. SGR meals are IAW the following: See SGR Meal calendar.
10. POC for Cadet Mess operations is Mr. George Witenko.  Please use the DOL Mess DISTRO when emailing any mess questions or traffic regarding mess.
AF-E= Ethnic mermite (meal consisting of lamb, rice, tea, etc) will be used for training purposes in CLDT
BOX = Boxed breakfast or lunch depending on the time
BULK = Typically consists of bagels/muffins, granola bars, fruit, milk, etc (as determined the the USCC Dietician)
Times listed are times the Cadets will be seated at the appropriate dining facility
Cadet Field Training/Cadet Leader Development Time (Okinawa Hall)

1. All MREs for CFT/CLDT will be drawn from the associated detail S4. The S4 shop is responsible for drawing and issuing the correct amount to each Company Supply Sergeant/White Cell.  Draw times will be coordinated by the S4 shop. The S4 is responsible for notifying the CST Sustainment Cell when the garage is half full IOT allow time for DOL to resupply
2. Breakfast pick up: MRE Supplements, boxed meals or mermites will be picked up from the loading deck at 0500
3. Breakfast containers: Turned in at next pickup
5. Dinner pick up: MRE supplements, boxed meals or mermites picked up from the loading dock at 1600
6. Dinner containers: Turned in NLT next meal pickup

7. When the entire CFT Regiment is scheduled to eat a meal at the same time, 8th Company will be served in Okinawa Hall. Reminder to Cadet Mess to ensure condiments are available at Barth Hall as well. If any company is out at training, 8th company will occupy that company's table at OH. 8th CO CDT Leadership needs to check the meal matrix for the Regiment to ensure they know where they will be served
8. All CFT meals are mandatory with assigned seating. Seating chart will be published by the CDT Staff during LTP
9. All CLDT and CFT LTP meals are mandatory with unassigned seating
10. Guests authorized on the access roster will be served either in Help's Hall or they can fill a hole at a Company table.
11. If all companies are in the field training, Okinawa Hall will be closed. Any Cadet or Guest not in the field can draw an MRE from the S4 shop
12. Standard meal times for CFT are 0830, 1230, and 1900. There are a few exceptions noted on the weekly calendars
1. Box Breakfast for ACFT Validation/Testing Mornings (Assumption)
2. CFF is M-M-A for CFT I Companies (Assumption due to compact training schedule)
3. BRM Package for all COs is A-M-A except for Day 3 which is M-M-A (Fact)
4. LN Package is A-M-A for Day 1 and M-M-A for Day 2 due to testing (Fact)
5. RSOI Days for both Cadre/Trainees is Box Breakfast (Assumption)
7. WTD Testing/ITP Transition Days are MRE Breakfast (Fact), MRE Lunch, Mermite Dinner
8. MACP/MED Days remain Mermite Breakfast and occurs between MACP and MED Training (Fact)
9. CFF Two-Day Package can be A-M-A (Fact)
10. Breakfast pick-up remains at 0530 from loading dock (Assumption)
11. ITP Day 2 is A-M-A, FTX Days 1-2 is M-M-M, FTX Day 3 is M-M-A (Fact)
12. How do we survey for special meal requirements? (RFI)

Everyone except CFT Cadre, CLDT 1, Wrestling PIAD (Mermites requested through AY channels on certain dates but eating in mess hall majority days), and Parachute will eat in mess hall.. but everyone on post is sitting with the corp between 18-24MAY
B = Boxed breakfast or lunch depending on the time
